mnemonics.title: Three Schema Architecture
mnemonics.content: ECI: External (User) - Conceptual (Logical) - Internal (Physical). Remember: 'Every Crazy Idea Needs Implementation'

mnemonics.title: ACID Properties
mnemonics.content: A-C-I-D: Atomic (All or Nothing), Consistent (Valid State), Isolated (Concurrent appears Serial), Durable (Permanent)

mnemonics.title: Database Evolution
mnemonics.content: File → Hierarchical → Network → Relational → Object → NoSQL. Remember: 'Harold Never Raved Over No Soup'

acronyms.usage: Software for creating and managing databases like Oracle, MySQL, PostgreSQL
Standard language for managing relational databases
Professional responsible for database management and security
Commands for defining database schema (CREATE, ALTER, DROP)
Commands for data operations (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)
Organization that defined network database model standards

visualTips.visualization: Draw three stacked boxes: Bottom = Internal (storage), Middle = Conceptual (logical), Top = External (user views). Show arrows between them representing independence.
Draw horizontal timeline: 1960s (File systems), 1969 (Hierarchical - IMS), 1970 (Codd's Relational Model), 1979 (Oracle SQL), 1980s-90s (RDBMS boom), 2000s (NoSQL).
Hierarchical: Tree diagram (one parent, multiple children). Network: Spider web/gaph (multiple connections, many-to-many).
Three circles labeled Insert, Update, Delete. Show how each causes problems when data is poorly organized.

analogies.analogy: Like a library with no catalog - each person keeps their own copy of books, leading to confusion when someone updates information.
Like a well-organized library with a librarian (DBMS) who manages all books centrally, prevents duplicates, and ensures everyone gets correct information.
Like a spreadsheet where each table is a separate sheet, connected through common columns (like employee ID linking tables).
Like the foundation of a building - changing the foundation (physical) doesn't affect the rooms (logical) or furniture (external).
